caseStudies.title: Real-Time Analytics at Scale
caseStudies.description: Powering sub-second analytics over petabyte-scale datasets.
caseStudies.overview: A digital advertising platform needed to provide advertisers with real-time campaign performance dashboards over datasets exceeding 10 petabytes. Existing query infrastructure could not meet sub-second latency requirements at the required concurrency levels.
caseStudies.problem: Advertiser-facing dashboards refreshed every 15 minutes and timed out under concurrent load. Campaigns could run for hours before advertisers detected performance issues, resulting in significant wasted spend and eroded platform trust.
caseStudies.solution: The query architecture was redesigned around a cloud data warehouse with aggressive materialised view strategies, result caching, and workload isolation for advertiser queries. Data ingestion was refactored to update materialised views incrementally in near-real-time as new event data arrived.

results.summary: Advertisers now access campaign performance data with seconds of latency, enabling real-time budget adjustments that improve campaign ROI. Platform NPS improved significantly following the launch.

caseStudies.title: Data Sharing Monetization
caseStudies.description: Launching a B2B data marketplace on a cloud platform.
caseStudies.overview: A weather data provider sought to monetize its proprietary datasets by enabling B2B customers to access live data directly in their own analytics environments, without the complexity of traditional data delivery via SFTP or APIs.
caseStudies.problem: Existing data delivery via API required customers to build and maintain ingestion pipelines, limiting the addressable market to technically sophisticated buyers. Revenue per customer was capped by API call pricing, which didn't reflect the full value customers derived from the data.
caseStudies.solution: A data sharing architecture was implemented that allowed customers to access live datasets directly within their own cloud accounts with zero-copy data transfer — no pipelines required. Usage-based pricing was introduced, with metering at the query level to capture value proportional to consumption.

results.summary: The data marketplace has opened an entirely new customer segment of business analysts who previously lacked the technical capability to consume the provider's data. It has become the primary growth driver for the business.

caseStudies.title: Collaborative Analytics for Healthcare
caseStudies.description: Secure multi-party data collaboration without data movement.
caseStudies.overview: A consortium of healthcare networks needed to collaborate on population health analytics across combined patient datasets, while ensuring strict compliance with data privacy regulations that prohibited raw data from leaving each organisation's controlled environment.
caseStudies.problem: Valuable population-level insights were locked within individual health network siloes. Research collaborations required lengthy legal negotiations and physical data transfers with complex de-identification processes, taking months to establish and limiting the scope of studies.
caseStudies.solution: A privacy-preserving collaboration architecture was implemented allowing each network to contribute to joint analyses without sharing raw patient records. Query results were the only data exchanged, with differential privacy techniques applied to prevent re-identification. Data governance controls ensured each participant could audit exactly what computations were performed on their data.

stats.label: Time to first joint analysis (down from 9 months)
New research studies enabled in year 1

results.summary: The consortium now conducts population health research at a scale previously impossible, unlocking insights that benefit patient outcomes across the network. The privacy-preserving architecture has become a template for future research collaborations.
